task_completion.description: Verify the food.gdt dataset is loaded into the econometrics software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main software window (e.g., gretl) shows 'food.gdt' as the active dataset, listing variables like 'food_exp' and 'income'.

task_completion.description: Estimate the linear OLS model of food expenditure on a constant and income.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A model output window showing an OLS regression with dependent variable 'food_exp' and independent variable 'income'.

task_completion.description: Perform Ramsey's RESET test using squared and cubed fitted values on the linear model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A test results window showing 'RESET test' or 'Ramsey's RESET' applied to the linear model, displaying test statistics and p-values.

task_completion.description: Generate log transformations for both food expenditure and income.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main variable list shows new log-transformed variables, typically named 'l_food_exp' and 'l_income' (or similar).

task_completion.description: Estimate the log-linear OLS model using the newly generated log variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A model output window showing an OLS regression with dependent variable 'l_food_exp' and independent variable 'l_income'.

task_completion.description: Save the estimation results for both models and the RESET test to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window showing the contents of 'reset_comparison.txt' at '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/', containing coefficients, standard errors, R-squared, and RESET test results for both models.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used actual econometrics software (like gretl) to compute the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the software's GUI or CLI being actively used to run the regressions and tests, rather than the agent just typing numbers into a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not copy-paste incorrect metadata values for the log-linear model.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final output file must contain the actual computed values for the log-linear model (intercept ~3.964, slope ~0.556, R-squared ~0.445) and NOT the incorrect metadata values (intercept 3.69, slope 0.38, R-squared 0.36).

integrity.description: Verify the output file is saved in the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor title bar shows the file is explicitly saved at '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/reset_comparison.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset: food.gdt (40 observations). Linear model correct values: intercept = 83.42, slope = 10.21, R-squared = 0.385. Log-linear model correct values: intercept = 3.964, slope (elasticity) = 0.556, R-squared = 0.445. The agent must output these actual computed values. If the agent outputs an elasticity of 0.38 or an intercept of 3.69 for the log-linear model, it has hallucinated or copied incorrect metadata.
